title: podman complains about XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, but doesn't appear to use the value of XDG_RUNTIME_DIR
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

kind bug

Podman is complaining about XDG_RUNTIME_DIR pointing to a path that is not writable, and then tries to access a different directory then what XDG_RUNTIME_DIR points to.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman info` or really any podman command

**Describe the results you received:**

Here I run env and podman info, and podman complains about  XDG_RUNTIME_DIR pointing to a not writable directory, but the actuall permission denied error doesn't refer to the directory XDG_RUNTIME_DIR points to.

```
∵ strange10 ~ ∴ sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait sh -c 'env && podman info'
SHELL=/usr/bin/zsh
PWD=/var/foo/home/kevin
LOGNAME=kevin
SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID=2477
HOME=/var/home/kevin
LANG=C.UTF-8
INVOCATION_ID=fb25e1905b854471b8dce7d233f7177a
MANAGERPID=1198
TERM=xterm-256color
USER=kevin
SHLVL=1
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1001
PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/sbin
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1001/bus
_=/usr/bin/env
WARN[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is pointing to a path which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail.
Error: error creating tmpdir: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
zsh: exit 125   sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait sh -
∵ strange10 ~ ∴
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I guess I expect that if podman is going to complain about XDG_RUNTIME_DIR not being writeable, that it is actually looking at the XDG_RUNTIME_DIR

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I moved my home directory to a new system and got a new uid, the uid was 1000 on the old system, is 1001 now. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
∵ strange10 ~ ∴ sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait podman version
WARN[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is pointing to a path which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail.
Error: error creating tmpdir: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
zsh: exit 125   sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait
∵ strange10 ~ ∴
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
∵ strange10 ~ ∴ sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait podman info --debug
WARN[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is pointing to a path which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail.
Error: error creating tmpdir: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
zsh: exit 125   sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait  inf
∵ strange10 ~ ∴
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
∵ strange10 ~ ∴ rpm -q podman
podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64
∵ strange10 ~ ∴
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes I have checked the troubleshooting guide. I am not even sure what the latest version of podman is, or even how I would get fedora core os to install it.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
moved to a newer version of podman, behavior is stil lthe same
```
∵ strange10 ~ ∴     rpm -q podman
podman-4.0.3-1.fc36.x86_64
∵ strange10 ~ ∴ sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait sh -c 'env && podman info'
SHELL=/usr/bin/zsh
PWD=/var/foo/home/kevin
LOGNAME=kevin
SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID=1689
HOME=/var/home/kevin
LANG=C.UTF-8
INVOCATION_ID=910e8d8ec1e0485ea5b753075c684633
MANAGERPID=1529
TERM=xterm-256color
USER=kevin
SHLVL=1
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1001
PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/sbin
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1001/bus
_=/usr/bin/env
WARN[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is pointing to a path which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail.
Error: error creating tmpdir: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
zsh: exit 125   sudo systemd-run --machine=kevin@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait sh -
∵ strange10 ~ ∴
```

according to this code here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/bdaac4b2b6573d0954c58da9968536017160b1ed/libpod/runtime.go#L351-L352

it looks like podman isn't reading XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, but looking at some kind of storage config.

here it looks like XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is being overwritten by something coming from some database somewhere
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/bdaac4b2b6573d0954c58da9968536017160b1ed/libpod/runtime.go#L1055-L1060

I can reproduce it on a Fedora 36 computer (running Podman 4.1.0).

Maybe Podman could try to detect that the UID was changed and provide a better error message?
```
[root@asus ~]# cat /etc/fedora-release 
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
[root@asus ~]# podman --version
podman version 4.1.0
[root@asus ~]# useradd test
[root@asus ~]# systemd-run --machine=test@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait sh -c 'podman version'
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri May  6 18:15:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@asus ~]# usermod -u 1039 test
[root@asus ~]# systemd-run --machine=test@ --quiet --user --collect --pipe --wait sh -c 'env && podman info'
SHELL=/bin/bash
PWD=/home/test
LOGNAME=test
SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID=6534
HOME=/home/test
LANG=en_US.UTF-8
INVOCATION_ID=7c5303e0fb2142d6bfe0ed40ebc772cf
MANAGERPID=6507
TERM=xterm-256color
USER=test
SHLVL=1
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1039
XDG_DATA_DIRS=/home/test/.local/share/flatpak/exports/share:/var/lib/flatpak/exports/share:/usr/local/share/:/usr/share/
PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/sbin
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1039/bus
_=/usr/bin/env
WARN[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is pointing to a path which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail. 
Error: error creating tmpdir: mkdir /run/user/1038: permission denied
[root@asus ~]# 
```

I opened a pr https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14255 that just changes the error message to not mention XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, since that is not what is being directly read, and instead mention RunRoot

(edited to correctly link to the pr)
Lets change the question, reopen if I was not supposed to close.
